programme.title: 999: Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The team joins the search for a missing aeroplane pilot and passenger. A man digging for antique bottles in a South Yorkshire tip is buried alive, leading to a desperate fight to save him.

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: When the body of a scientist is found in Loch Katrine, Annika is reminded of Ibsens Enemy of the People. But with so many people wanting him dead, which one did he push over the edge?

programme.title: Annika
programme.desc: When a body is found on a boat in the Clyde, the case takes the team to the Isle of Bute. Evidence points to a jilted lover, but as the team investigate, a darker secret starts to emerge.

programme.title: Searching For Satoshi: The Mysterious Disappearance of the Bitcoin Creator
Katie Piper: Locked Up in Louisiana
programme.desc: Katie meets Candice who is facing a second-degree murder charge, and learns that she was born into a gang and has grown up around violence - is it too late for her to change?

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.desc: Before his passing, Kansas City Chiefs superfan Landan wanted to help his wife Tiffany and requested that the Death Cleaners help her move forward and clean his mancave.

programme.title: Deepfake Porn: You Could Be Next
programme.desc: Most people think of deepfakes as manipulated video clips of world leaders, designed to create confusion, but the most pressing threat of deepfakes is not politics, but porn. Research shows around 96 per cent of deepfake videos are pornographic, with almost 100 per cent of them involving non-consenting women. The film features three women who have been impacted by this form of image-based sexual abuse.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: It might be Christmas, but Noel Fitzpatrick is busier than ever. Adam, a sleigh-pulling Alaskan malamute, needs both knees replacing with implants, and there is a six-week-old miniature dachshund puppy with a deformed foot.

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: An 18-week-old cockapoo puppy Henry has a fractured neck following a freak garden accident. Professor Noel Fitzpatrick is forced to perform a risky, and truly inventive, procedure to realign the fracture in an attempt to save his life.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Homeowners across Britain try to complete do-it-yourself home projects on their own. But sometimes, they need help finishing the work. Thats where presenter Nick Knowles and his team of designers and builders step in. They help homeowners complete their DIY projects gone awry.

programme.title: DIY SOS: The Big Build
programme.desc: Homeowners across Britain try to complete do-it-yourself home projects on their own. But sometimes, they need help finishing the work. Thats where presenter Nick Knowles and his team of designers and builders step in. They help homeowners complete their DIY projects gone awry.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Pete returns to Dogs Trust, facing a suitcase of abandoned pups on day one and falling hard for Theo, a gentle Italian Mastiff with a serious heart condition.

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: Katie was separated from her boyfriend when she discovered her pregnancy but reunited at the first scan. Nicole is admitted with a significant bleed, and Jolanta is hoping her third child will be born underwater.

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: Two couples prove that unplanned pregnancies can happen at very different ages. Andy, whos 41, and Nina, whos 39, thought theyd long left nappies and sleepless nights behind. Jonathan, whos 21, met 18-year-old Laura when he randomly sent her a message on a social networking site. Three months later she fell pregnant.

programme.title: Emergency: First Time Medics
programme.desc: The junior doctors are now in their second week at the Royal Berkshire Hospital. Adrian does a tricky blood test on a critically-ill patient, and Tej spots some worrying test results.

programme.title: Emergency: First Time Medics
programme.desc: Its the night shift for the junior doctors. Adrians ego takes a hit as he bites off more than he can chew, and Ese must solve the mystery of a patients unexplained collapse.

programme.title: Louis Theroux Interviews...
programme.desc: Louis spends time with platinum-selling rapper Stormzy. After two years out of the spotlight, Stormzy welcomes Louis into his home in south west London to sit down for a frank and revealing conversation that covers many aspects of his life, from his childhood to the breakdown of a high-profile relationship, as well as the pressures that come with being one of the countrys best-loved musicians and role models.

programme.title: Dangerous Breed: Crime. Cons. Cats.
programme.desc: In 2012, Teddy Hart -- an eccentric, cat-breeding pro-wrestler living in Canada -- teams up with filmmaker Frederick Kroetsch to make a reality show; the show takes a dark turn when two women make disturbing allegations and Teddy leaves for the U.S.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: From Wakefield, West Yorkshire, Michael visits the National Coal Mining Museum for England at Caphouse Colliery. He pauses to admire the tall spire of Wakefield Cathedral and its resident peregrine falcons.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his post-war exploration of north west England in Bradford, Shipley and Hebden Bridge. In Centenary Square in Bradford, Michael encounters Bradfords literary giant JB Priestley, author of An Inspector Calls.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels through the Britain of his youth from Londons Docklands and East End to the city within a city, the Barbican.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his railway exploration of the post-war Britain of his youth on a journey from London to Cambridge.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave collects a Ford Fiesta XR2i thats not moved from a garage for twenty-one years and Derek picks up a 1956 Siva Tourer, made famous in Doctor Who.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Dave meets the inspirational owner of a Reliant Robin whos selling it to raise money for charity.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: The Japanese, at war on the Chinese mainland since 1931, hope for quick victories over the British and the Dutch, but 7 December 1941 brings the infamous attack on Pearl Harbor, and the start of an all-conquering sweep through Southeast Asia culminating in the taking of Singapore.

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: The Germans hear that Polish forces are fighting alongside a huge bear. Tales of this beer-swilling, chain-smoking beast terrify them, but incredibly the stories are true.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Filmed on location at the historic St Thomas and St Bartholomews hospitals in London, the programme features fascinating objects that provide insight into the history of nursing and healthcare, from medals awarded for bravery to early uniforms, cartoons and medical equipment. Fiona Bruce meets comedian Jo Brand, a former psychiatric nurse, and hears about her experience working in emergency mental health clinics, as well lighter moments performing in hospital pantos.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: In this special episode, filmed before the Accession, Fiona introduces the Queen Consort to roadshow visitors and members of the team of experts, and finds out about her close connection to the Eden Project and the charity The Big Lunch.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A 1969 Morris Mini Cooper MkII is totally restored by specialists. They give it the full works, which means only one thing... any chance of profit heads towards an open window.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The Restoring Classics team set out on a Pre-War challenge with a 1939 three wheeled Morgan F4 - and aptly named Russ Storer from MG Mecca is still keen to take it on.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: The Japanese, at war on the Chinese mainland since 1931, hope for quick victories over the British and the Dutch, but 7 December 1941 brings the infamous attack on Pearl Harbor, and the start of an all-conquering sweep through Southeast Asia culminating in the taking of Singapore.

programme.title: World War Weird
programme.desc: The Germans hear that Polish forces are fighting alongside a huge bear. Tales of this beer-swilling, chain-smoking beast terrify them, but incredibly the stories are true.

Fiona Bruce meets comedian Jo Brand, a former psychiatric nurse, and hears about her experience working in emergency mental health clinics, as well lighter moments performing in hospital pantos.

programme.title: Mediterranean With Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Taking a ferry across the Strait of Gibraltar, Simons first stop is Ceuta, a Spanish exclave surrounded by Morocco. This is one of the few land borders between Africa and the European Union. Simon joins the Spanish border police who check engines and even dashboards for stowaways trying to reach Europe.

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: Expert hauliers Eric Harrison and Chris Case move a Deltic diesel, better known as The Royal Highland Fusilier, halfway across the country to the Telford Steam Fair.

programme.title: David Jasons Great British Inventions
programme.desc: Sir David gets behind the controls of a tank as he finds out about the British inventors who gave us some of the most extreme machines on the planet.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien are in the West Midlands and London, with finds including a potato oven and an old petrol pump.

programme.title: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley examines Agatha Christies later life, and discovers how, amid the turbulent social and political change of the 1930s and 40s, newfound personal happiness ushered in a golden age for her writing.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores the post-war Britain of his youth in a journey from the Midlands to the West Country. Beginning in Derbys railway works, Michael hears how the Victorian sheds now house some of the most modern assembly lines in Britain.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: America entered conflict after the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor, but the conflict in Europe and the conflict in the Pacific were two separate wars. Many Americans would have been content to forget about Hitler and Europe. Despite opposition from congress President Roosevelt, however, was committed to fighting Hitler. Eventually Hitler declared war on America, thus relieving Roosevelt of a difficult decision.
